title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: longitudinal DIM, predictors decomposed within-person -person components dyadic decomposition (Bolger Laurenceau 2013; Gistelinck Loeys 2020). default "auto" selects "time_2l" time predictors supplied. also retains raw dyad-occasion means within-dyad member deviations. decomposed columns used : cwp dyad mean captures shared occasion-specific shift two members’ usual levels (shared occasion-level variation). cwp within-dyad member deviation captures member usual level occasion. cbp dyad mean captures dyad’s shared usual level relative sample’s grand mean (stable -dyad differences). cbp within-dyad member deviation captures member’s stable difference dyad’s usual level. cbp terms use member’s mean across observed occasions estimate member’s longer-run usual level. occasions (small TT), especially predictor low stability time, person means can unreliable. associated -person estimates can therefore biased imprecise, interpreted cautiously (Gottfredson 2019). Use temporal_predictor_decomposition = "none" construct raw dyad-occasion mean within-dyad member deviation. example estimates -day associations support closeness includes diaryday adjust linear trend across study.

what: Interpretation of concurrent ILD DIM coefficients
title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: mean--deviation interpretation applies longitudinally. Coefficients dyad means describe expected individual outcomes expected couple-average outcomes. Coefficients within-dyad member deviations describe members’ deviations expected difference. cbp dyad-mean estimate (1.51) means , comparing couples whose average usual support differs one point holding stable difference partners constant, expected couple-average closeness 1.51 points higher higher-support couple. Equivalently, member expected report 1.51 points higher closeness. cwp dyad-mean estimate (0.49) means members one point respective usual support levels, member’s expected closeness 0.49 points higher usual levels, holding difference momentary deviations constant. Equivalently, expected couple-average closeness 0.49 points higher occasion. cbp within-dyad member-deviation estimate (0.78) means partners differ one point usual support levels, expected differ 0.78 points closeness, holding couple’s average usual support predictors constant. member terms, suppose one member 0.5 points couple’s average usual support 0.5 points . expected closeness 0.39 points couple’s predicted mean, respectively. cwp within-dyad member-deviation estimate (0.06) means one partner’s momentary deviation usual support one point higher partner’s deviation, expected differ 0.06 points closeness, holding occasion-specific dyad mean predictors constant. member terms, suppose momentary deviations 0.5 points occasion-specific dyad mean. expected closeness 0.03 points couple’s predicted mean, respectively.

what: Equivalence of APIM and DIM in ILD
title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: equivalent APIM uses actor partner effects levels: two ILD models identical fit statistics: equivalence holds separately within-person (cwp) -person (cbp) predictor components. within-person component: bcwp,mean=bcwp,actor+bcwp,partner b_{\mathrm{cwp,mean}} = b_{\mathrm{cwp,actor}} + b_{\mathrm{cwp,partner}} bcwp,diff=bcwp,actor−bcwp,partner b_{\mathrm{cwp,diff}} = b_{\mathrm{cwp,actor}} - b_{\mathrm{cwp,partner}} -person component: bcbp,mean=bcbp,actor+bcbp,partner b_{\mathrm{cbp,mean}} = b_{\mathrm{cbp,actor}} + b_{\mathrm{cbp,partner}} bcbp,diff=bcbp,actor−bcbp,partner b_{\mathrm{cbp,diff}} = b_{\mathrm{cbp,actor}} - b_{\mathrm{cbp,partner}} also means APIM parameterization can used one level DIM parameterization . example: mixed parameterization still estimates model:
code: apim_ILD <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   closeness ~     1 +      diaryday +      # Within-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cwp_partner +      # Between-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cbp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cbp_partner +      # Stable exchangeable dyad-level covariance     us(1 | coupleID)  + us(0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # Residual (same-day) exchangeable dyad-level covariance     us(1 | coupleID:diaryday) +     us(0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_exchangeable_data ) data.frame(   model = c("DIM", "APIM"),   AIC = c(AIC(dim_ILD), AIC(apim_ILD)),   BIC = c(BIC(dim_ILD), BIC(apim_ILD)),   logLik = c(as.numeric(logLik(dim_ILD)), as.numeric(logLik(apim_ILD))) ) #>   model      AIC      BIC    logLik #> 1   DIM 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 2  APIM 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 apim_dim_ILD <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   closeness ~     1 +      diaryday +      # Within-person APIM     .i_provided_support_cwp_actor +     .i_provided_support_cwp_partner +      # Between-person DIM     .i_provided_support_cbp_dyad_mean +     .i_provided_support_cbp_within_dyad_dev +      # Stable exchangeable dyad-level covariance     us(1 | coupleID)  + us(0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # Same-day exchangeable dyad-level covariance     us(1 | coupleID:diaryday) +     us(0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_exchangeable_data ) data.frame(   model = c("DIM within / DIM between", "APIM within / APIM between", "APIM within / DIM between"),   AIC = c(AIC(dim_ILD), AIC(apim_ILD), AIC(apim_dim_ILD)),   BIC = c(BIC(dim_ILD), BIC(apim_ILD), BIC(apim_dim_ILD)),   logLik = c(     as.numeric(logLik(dim_ILD)),     as.numeric(logLik(apim_ILD)),     as.numeric(logLik(apim_dim_ILD))   ) ) #>                        model      AIC      BIC    logLik #> 1   DIM within / DIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 2 APIM within / APIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613 #> 3  APIM within / DIM between 2977.225 3026.637 -1478.613

title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: Random slopes can included DIM adding corresponding within-person effects stable dyad-level random-effect blocks: following syntax illustrates full random-slope specification. fitted example data support complex random-effects structure. first stable dyad-level random effects block contains shared DIM intercept, dyad-mean slope, within-dyad member-deviation slope. second block contains member-difference counterparts, included .i_diff_* interactions. uncorrelated blocks allow two members different random slopes preserving exchangeability.
code: dim_ILD_random <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   closeness ~     1 +      diaryday +      # Within-person DIM     .i_provided_support_cwp_dyad_mean +     .i_provided_support_cwp_within_dyad_dev +      # Between-person DIM     .i_provided_support_cbp_dyad_mean +     .i_provided_support_cbp_within_dyad_dev +      # Stable dyad-level covariance with within-person random slopes     us(1 +        .i_provided_support_cwp_dyad_mean +        .i_provided_support_cwp_within_dyad_dev      | coupleID)  +     us(0 +        .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary +        .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary:.i_provided_support_cwp_dyad_mean +        .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary:.i_provided_support_cwp_within_dyad_dev      | coupleID) +      # Same-day exchangeable dyad-level covariance     us(1 | coupleID:diaryday) +     us(0 + .i_diff_assumed_exchangeable_arbitrary | coupleID:diaryday)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = ild_exchangeable_data )

what: Transforming DIM random slopes to APIM slopes
title: Dyad-Individual Model
text: Applying transformation random-slope coefficients proceeds two steps. First, transform DIM dyad-mean within-dyad member-deviation random slopes APIM actor partner random slopes. Random effects use uu, subscripts write actor, partner, mean, dev. shared block, uactor,j=umean,j+udev,j2,upartner,j=umean,j−udev,j2, u_{\mathrm{actor},j} = \frac{u_{\mathrm{mean},j} + u_{\mathrm{dev},j}}{2}, \qquad u_{\mathrm{partner},j} = \frac{u_{\mathrm{mean},j} - u_{\mathrm{dev},j}}{2}, .i_diff_* block, marked tilde, ũactor,j=ũmean,j+ũdev,j2,ũpartner,j=ũmean,j−ũdev,j2. \widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{actor},j} = \frac{\widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{mean},j} + \widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{dev},j}}{2}, \qquad \widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{partner},j} = \frac{\widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{mean},j} - \widetilde{u}_{\mathrm{dev},j}}{2}. shared .i_diff_* random intercepts remain unchanged. now shared .i_diff_* actor partner effects back-transformed complete readily interpretable member-specific actor-partner covariance matrix. described exchangeable random-slope back-transformation APIM vignette.

text: Let bactor,femaleb_{\mathrm{actor},\mathrm{female}} bactor,maleb_{\mathrm{actor},\mathrm{male}} denote actor effects female male outcomes. corresponding partner effects bpartner,femaleb_{\mathrm{partner},\mathrm{female}} bpartner,maleb_{\mathrm{partner},\mathrm{male}}, APIM intercepts b0,femaleb_{0,\mathrm{female}} b0,maleb_{0,\mathrm{male}}. Fixed APIM coefficients use bb write effect outcome role. numbered paths a10a_{10} a22a_{22} retain published DSM notation. slope transformation can understood two steps. First, outcome role r∈{female,male}r \\{\mathrm{female},\mathrm{male}\}, form actor-plus-partner actor-minus-partner combinations: bsum,r=bactor,r+bpartner,r,bdifference,r=bactor,r−bpartner,r. \begin{aligned} b_{\mathrm{sum},r} &= b_{\mathrm{actor},r} + b_{\mathrm{partner},r}, \\ b_{\mathrm{difference},r} &= b_{\mathrm{actor},r} - b_{\mathrm{partner},r}. \end{aligned} DSM slopes follow combining female- male-outcome effects: a11=bsum,female+bsum,male2,a21=bsum,female−bsum,male,a12=bdifference,female−bdifference,male4,a22=bdifference,female+bdifference,male2. \begin{aligned} a_{11} &= \frac{b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{female}}         + b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{male}}}{2}, & a_{21} &= b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{female}}    - b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{male}}, \\ a_{12} &= \frac{b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{female}}         - b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{male}}}{4}, & a_{22} &= \frac{b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{female}}         + b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{male}}}{2}. \end{aligned} APIM predictors retain original scale, whereas DSM predictor level grand-mean centered. Keeping raw APIM predictors original scale preserves reference values; centering difference handled explicitly intercept transformation. μX\mu_X grand mean subtracted DSM predictor level, intercepts transform a10=b0,female+b0,male2+μXa11,a20=b0,female−b0,male+μXa21. a_{10} = \frac{b_{0,\mathrm{female}} + b_{0,\mathrm{male}}}{2} + \mu_X a_{11}, \qquad a_{20} = b_{0,\mathrm{female}} - b_{0,\mathrm{male}} + \mu_X a_{21}. reverse slope transformation, first recover role-specific actor-plus-partner actor-minus-partner combinations: bsum,female=a11+a212,bsum,male=a11−a212,bdifference,female=a22+2a12,bdifference,male=a22−2a12. \begin{aligned} b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{female}} &= a_{11} + \frac{a_{21}}{2}, & b_{\mathrm{sum},\mathrm{male}} &= a_{11} - \frac{a_{21}}{2}, \\ b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{female}} &= a_{22} + 2a_{12}, & b_{\mathrm{difference},\mathrm{male}} &= a_{22} - 2a_{12}. \end{aligned} , outcome role rr, bactor,r=bsum,r+bdifference,r2,bpartner,r=bsum,r−bdifference,r2. b_{\mathrm{actor},r} = \frac{b_{\mathrm{sum},r} + b_{\mathrm{difference},r}}{2}, \qquad b_{\mathrm{partner},r} = \frac{b_{\mathrm{sum},r} - b_{\mathrm{difference},r}}{2}. intercepts transform back b0,female=a10+a202−μX(a11+a212), b_{0,\mathrm{female}} = a_{10} + \frac{a_{20}}{2} - \mu_X\left(a_{11} + \frac{a_{21}}{2}\right), b0,male=a10−a202−μX(a11−a212). b_{0,\mathrm{male}} = a_{10} - \frac{a_{20}}{2} - \mu_X\left(a_{11} - \frac{a_{21}}{2}\right). following comparison applies APIM--DSM transformation six fixed effects: APIM--DSM fixed-effect transformation (centering constant = 5.148).

title: Getting Started
text: example_dyadic_ILD simulated intensive longitudinal dyadic dataset. dyad repeated observations diaryday, one row per person-day. prepare intensive longitudinal data, pass time variable prepare_interdep_data(). default, numeric predictors longitudinal APIM preparation decomposed within-person -person components (Bolger Laurenceau 2013). temporal predictor decomposition controlled temporal_predictor_decomposition. default "auto" setting selects "time_2l" longitudinal setup retains raw actor partner columns alongside components. Note observed person means used construct -person (cbp) predictors can unreliable member contributes occasions, can bias -person estimates (Gottfredson 2019).

title: Getting Started
text: Lagged versions variables, including outcome also passed predictors, can obtained lag_predictors argument. prepare_interdep_data() returns lag-1 raw within-person-centered actor partner columns alongside contemporaneous versions. Lagging respects dyad member structure, matches observations exactly time - 1, bridge missing occasions. Brief example: Note: Whether use raw within-person-centered lagged outcome depends research question data. Including lagged outcome dynamic models can introduce bias, especially shorter time series (Hamaker Grasman 2015; Nickell 1981; Gistelinck et al. 2021). See APIM vignette detailed discussion guidance.

code: mixed_cross_data_constrained <- prepare_interdep_data(   example_dyadic_crosssectional_mixed,   group = coupleID,   member = personID,   role = gender,   set_exchangeable_compositions = "male-female",   pool_compositions = list(     "non_female_x_female" = c("male-male", "female-male")   ),   seed = 123 )  mixed_cross_gaussian_model_constrained <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(   satisfaction ~ 0 +     .i_is_female_x_female +     .i_is_non_female_x_female +      # exchangeable female-female residual covariance via sum-diff     us(0 + .i_is_female_x_female | coupleID) +     us(0 + .i_diff_female_x_female_arbitrary | coupleID) +      # pooled female-male and male-male residual covariance via sum-diff     us(0 + .i_is_non_female_x_female | coupleID) +     us(0 + .i_diff_non_female_x_female_arbitrary | coupleID)    , dispformula = ~ 0   , family = gaussian()   , data = mixed_cross_data_constrained )  compare_interdep_models(   mixed_cross_gaussian_model_constrained,   mixed_cross_gaussian_model ) #> Likelihood-ratio test for nested models fitted to equivalent data #> Assumes mathematical nesting and an appropriate chi-squared reference distribution. #>  #>                                        Df    AIC    BIC   logLik deviance #> mixed_cross_gaussian_model_constrained  6 2087.8 2114.6 -1037.90   2075.8 #> mixed_cross_gaussian_model             11 2010.0 2059.0  -993.97   1988.0 #>                                         Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)     #> mixed_cross_gaussian_model_constrained                              #> mixed_cross_gaussian_model             87.856      5  < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Conclusion (5% level): The likelihood-ratio test provides evidence that `mixed_cross_gaussian_model` fits better than `mixed_cross_gaussian_model_constrained` (p < 0.001).

text: Data must long format. Cross-sectional dyadic data may contain one row per member within dyad. Intensive longitudinal dyadic data may contain one row per member observed measurement occasion within dyad. Measured variables may contain missing values. Missing incomplete structural information controlled incomplete_dyads missing_role. Dyad composition labels canonical: role labels sorted alphabetically combined, labels depend row member order.

title: Validate dyadic input data — validate_interdep_data
text: data long-format data frame tibble. group Column identifying dyad. member Column identifying two people members within dyad, person ID. role Optional column identifying role can used distinguish partners within dyad, gender. role supplied, dyads data treated exchangeable. time Optional column identifying time measurement order. predictors Optional variables select store metadata temporal predictor decomposition model-helper functions. lag_predictors Optional subset predictors lag-1 model-ready columns created. Requires finite, integer-valued numeric time variable. model_type Requested model-ready column families. Can contain one "apim", "dim", "dsm". "none" indicates model-specific predictor construction must used alone. dsm_role_order model_type = "dsm", character vector giving two distinguishable roles order used directional differences. Required DSM columns requested must NULL otherwise. temporal_predictor_decomposition Requested temporal predictor decomposition strategy predictors. "none" leaves predictors undecomposed model-specific columns constructed. "time_2l" indicates two-level temporal predictor decomposition within-person -person components. "auto" resolves "time_2l" time predictors supplied, "none" otherwise. Model-specific helpers may apply additional conventions, grand-mean centering raw DIM DSM dyad means. incomplete_dyads handle dyads contain exactly two unique members anywhere data. "error" stops error "drop" removes entire dyad. missing_role handle missing values role column. "error" stops error, "drop" removes dyads incomplete role information. Ignored role column supplied.
